revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine
所澤神明社 | image = Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg | caption = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine During New Years | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|38.83|N|139|27|48.01|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1-2-4 Miyamoto-chō, Tokorozawa, Saitama, 359-1143 | website = {{URL|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tokorozawa Shimei Shrine'''|所澤神明社|Tokorosawa shinmei-sha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tokorozawa, Saitama]], Japan. == History == Tradition holds that the Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine was founded in 110 CE by [[Yamato Takeru]] during his conquest of eastern Japan and the shrine claims to have been one of the most important centers for the worship of [[Amaterasu]] in the [[Kantō region]]; however, all records were lost in a fire in 1826. With the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] {{Shinmei shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine
所澤神明社 | image = [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] | caption = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine During New Years | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|38.83|N|139|27|48.01|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1-2-4 Miyamoto-chō, Tokorozawa, Saitama, 359-1143 | website = {{URL|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tokorozawa Shimei Shrine'''|所澤神明社|Tokorosawa shinmei-sha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tokorozawa, Saitama]], Japan. == History == Tradition holds that the Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine was founded in 110 CE by [[Yamato Takeru]] during his conquest of eastern Japan and the shrine claims to have been one of the most important centers for the worship of [[Amaterasu]] in the [[Kantō region]]; however, all records were lost in a fire in 1826. With the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia04]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia24]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] {{Shinmei shrines}}

With the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[category:kk]][[category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinmei shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] {{Shinmei shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine
所澤神明社 | image = [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] | caption = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine During New Years | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|38.83|N|139|27|48.01|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1-2-4 Miyamoto-chō, Tokorozawa, Saitama, 359-1143 | website = {{URL|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tokorozawa Shimei Shrine'''|所澤神明社|Tokorosawa shinmei-sha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tokorozawa, Saitama]], Japan. == History == Tradition holds that the Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine was founded in 110 CE by [[Yamato Takeru]] during his conquest of eastern Japan and the shrine claims to have been one of the most important centers for the worship of [[Amaterasu]] in the [[Kantō region]]; however, all records were lost in a fire in 1826. With the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[category:Shinmei shrines]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:mass editing for the third subcategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] {{Shinmei shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine
所澤神明社 | image = [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] | caption = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine During New Years | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|38.83|N|139|27|48.01|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1-2-4 Miyamoto-chō, Tokorozawa, Saitama, 359-1143 | website = {{URL|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tokorozawa Shimei Shrine'''|所澤神明社|Tokorosawa shinmei-sha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tokorozawa, Saitama]], Japan. == History == Tradition holds that the Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine was founded in 110 CE by [[Yamato Takeru]] during his conquest of eastern Japan and the shrine claims to have been one of the most important centers for the worship of [[Amaterasu]] in the [[Kantō region]]; however, all records were lost in a fire in 1826. With the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] {{Shinmei shrines}}

With the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] {{Shinmei shrines}} [[Category:New]]

With the establishment of the [[Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:mass editing for the third subCategory]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] {{Shinmei shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine
所澤神明社 | image = | caption = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine During New Years | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|47|38.83|N|139|27|48.01|E|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1-2-4 Miyamoto-chō, Tokorozawa, Saitama, 359-1143 | website = {{URL|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tokorozawa Shimei Shrine'''|所澤神明社|Tokorosawa shinmei-sha}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tokorozawa, Saitama]], Japan. == History == Tradition holds that the Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine was founded in 110 CE by [[Yamato Takeru]] during his conquest of eastern Japan and the shrine claims to have been one of the most important centers for the worship of [[Amaterasu]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]; however, all records were lost in a fire in 1826. With the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinmei shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] [[sn:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine}} [[eu:Tokorozawa Shinmei]] [[fr:Tokorosawa shinmei-sha]] [[ja:所澤神明社]]

With the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] [[sn:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine}} [[eu:Tokorozawa Shinmei]] [[fr:Tokorosawa shinmei-sha]] [[ja:所澤神明社]]

所澤神明社 | image =File:Tokorosawa Shinmei-sha 100117.jpg | caption = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine During New Years | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.7941|N|139.463|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1-2-4 Miyamoto-chō, Tokorozawa, Saitama, 359-1143 | website = {{URL|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Tokorozawa Shimei Shrine'''|所澤神明社|Tokorosawa shinmei-sha}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa, Saitama]], Japan. == History == Tradition holds that the Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine was founded in 110 CE by [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] during his conquest of eastern Japan and the shrine claims to have been one of the most important centers for the worship of [[Amaterasu]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]; however, all records were lost in a fire in 1826. With the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine}} [[eu:Tokorozawa Shinmei]] [[fr:Tokorosawa shinmei-sha]] [[ja:所澤神明社]] {{wikidata link|Q288248}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

With the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine}} [[eu:Tokorozawa Shinmei]] [[fr:Tokorosawa shinmei-sha]] [[ja:所澤神明社]] {{wikidata link|Q288248}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q288248}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

With the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine}} [[eu:Tokorozawa Shinmei]] [[fr:Tokorosawa shinmei-sha]] [[ja:所澤神明社]] {{wikidata link|Q288248}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q288248}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

所澤神明社 | image =File:Tokorosawa Shinmei-sha 100117.jpg | caption = Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine During New Years | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.7941|N|139.463|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei Shrine]] | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1-2-4 Miyamoto-chō, Tokorozawa, Saitama, 359-1143 | website = {{URL|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tokorozawa|WD=Q165857}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Tokorozawa Shimei Shrine'''|所澤神明社|Tokorosawa shinmei-sha}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa, Saitama]], Japan. == History == Tradition holds that the Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine was founded in 110 CE by [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] during his conquest of eastern Japan and the shrine claims to have been one of the most important centers for the worship of [[Amaterasu]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]; however, all records were lost in a fire in 1826. With the establishment of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot [[:en:Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa]] began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] }} [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine}} [[eu:Tokorozawa Shinmei]] [[fr:Tokorosawa shinmei-sha]] [[ja:所澤神明社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q288248}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

With the establishment of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}}’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|WD=Q5694703|lt=Yoshitoshi Tokugawa}} began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinmei shrines}} [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] {{Commons category|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine}} [[eu:Tokorozawa Shinmei]] [[fr:Tokorosawa shinmei-sha]] [[ja:所澤神明社]] {{wikidata link|Q288248}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

With the establishment of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}’s Tokorozawa Air Field in 1911, pioneering pilot {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|WD=Q5694703|lt=Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|1=Yoshitoshi Tokugawa}} began a tradition of praying at this shrine for air safety. ==Enshrined kami== *[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] *[[Ukanomitama]] *[[Mount Miwa#Religious significance|Mount Miwa]] == Festivals == The major [[Japanese festivals]] of the shrine are: * January 1 – Gantan-sai (New Year’s Festival) * February 3 – [[Setsubun]] (traditional beginning of spring) * April 21 – Chi-sai * First Sunday in June – Ningyō-kuyō (Doll’s Day) * June 30 - Nagoshi-no Ō-harae (Summer Purification Ceremony) * September 15 – Tai-sai ==External links== {{commons Category}} *{{official|http://www.shinmeisha.or.jp/}} {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinmei shrines}} [[file:Tokorozawa-Shinmei-Shrine.jpg]] [[en:Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine]] {{Commons category|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine}} [[eu:Tokorozawa Shinmei]] [[fr:Tokorosawa shinmei-sha]] [[ja:所澤神明社]] {{wikidata link|Q288248}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
